title: Fix: inline task tier classification at creation (eliminate race condition)
context: model_tier is currently set in a fire-and-forget background task (asyncio.create_task). The orchestrator often picks up the task before it commits, leaving model_tier=None. Fix: call classify_task_tier() synchronously inline before db.flush() in POST /api/tasks. Remove the asyncio.create_task block. See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md (Fix 1). The classify_task_tier() service already exists in app/services/task_tier.py with 2s LM Studio timeout and correct fallback logic.
acceptance: 1. POST /api/tasks returns task with non-null model_tier. 2. Caller-supplied model_tier in body is preserved unchanged. 3. LM Studio down → model_tier='standard' within ~2s. 4. project_id containing 'lobs-server' + LLM returns 'small' → model_tier='standard' (hard minimum). 5. No asyncio.create_task in tasks.py after the fix.

title: Fix: ModelChooser skip decide_models() when explicit tier is set
context: When task.model_tier is already set (the normal case after Fix 1), ModelChooser.choose() still calls decide_models() and then overrides its output with tier_candidates. The decide_models() call is wasted work. Fix: when explicit_tier is set and tier_candidates exist, skip decide_models() entirely. Create a minimal _ExplicitTierDecision dataclass with .models, .criticality='standard', .complexity='medium', .audit={tier, tier_source, tier_models}. See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md (Fix 2). IMPORTANT: tier->model mapping must be read from DB settings (existing keys: MODEL_ROUTER_TIER_SMALL_KEY, etc.) — do NOT hardcode model lists.
acceptance: 1. When task.model_tier='standard': audit dict has tier_source='explicit', decide_models() not called. 2. When task.model_tier=None: falls through to decide_models() unchanged. 3. Budget guards, health ranking, routing policy all still run. 4. Tier list empty in DB → falls through to heuristic (existing behavior). 5. ModelChoice.budget_lane still populated correctly.

title: Fix: delete reflection cycle sessions after completion
context: spawn_reflection_agents() in workflow_reflection.py spawns Gateway sessions via worker_manager._spawn_session() but never deletes them. Sessions accumulate in Gateway. Fix requires two changes: (A) In spawn_reflection_agents(), add 'session_key': result.get('childSessionKey') to each spawn_results entry. (B) In check_reflections_complete(), when remaining==0, iterate spawn_results from context['spawn_agents']['spawn_results'], call worker_manager.gateway.delete_session(key) for each entry with a session_key. Delete failures must be non-fatal (log warning, continue). See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md (Fix 3).
acceptance: 1. spawn_results entries include session_key field. 2. When check_reflections_complete returns completed=True, all reflection session keys are deleted from Gateway. 3. Gateway delete failure logs warning but does not raise. 4. Return dict includes sessions_cleaned count. 5. No change to reflection cycle workflow seeds needed.
